name: Berbena
description: Seasonal, local ingredients come together with great precision at this Gràcia restaurant; the kitchen works in perfect harmony with its neighbourhood feel.

name: Âme
description: Blends French culinary technique with Mediterranean flavours in a warm, welcoming Eixample Esquerra setting.

name: Besta
description: Creative seafood dishes paired with a strong natural wine selection make this Eixample Esquerra spot essential.

name: Suru Bar
description: Unusual ingredient combinations that somehow remain approachable and exciting; one of Eixample Esquerra's most interesting new tables.

name: Franca
description: Reworks traditional Catalan dishes in compelling modern ways; one of the most exciting special-occasion restaurants in Eixample Dreta.

name: El Rectangle
description: Young, exciting cooking described as thrilling and approachable; captures the energy of the Sant Antoni dining scene right now.

name: Bardeni
description: Chef-driven approach to meat with quality and technique at the forefront; a serious address for carnivores in Eixample Dreta.

name: Maleducat
description: Captures the energy and spirit of Barcelona right now; one of the most alive spots in Sant Antoni's buzzing dining scene.

name: Alapar
description: Confident cooking with clear direction combines two cuisines—Mediterranean and Japanese—with impressive, cohesive results in Poble Sec.

name: Contracorrent Bar & Bistro
description: Passion-driven Italian ownership with an excellent natural wine focus; one of the most personal and welcoming neighbourhood bistros near Arc de Triomf.

name: Mikan
description: North-east Asian lunch boxes and small plates paired with low-intervention natural wines in a relaxed Eixample Esquerra setting.

name: Masa Vins
description: 'Your living room but with a fridge full of natural wines'—the most relaxed and welcoming natural wine bar in Poblenou.

name: Ona
description: An intimate six-seat venue with a constantly changing tasting menu; one of Barcelona's most singular and personal dining experiences.

name: Fishology
description: Uses a nose-to-tail fish philosophy with age-old fish cooking methods revived for a modern Eixample Esquerra audience.

name: Fonda Pepa
description: Catalan classics sprinkled with Mexican-inspired surprises; a genuinely original combination that works brilliantly in this Gràcia setting.

name: Lluritu
description: Straightforward yet flavourful seafood dishes in a casual Gràcia spot beloved by locals; no fuss, all flavour.

name: La Cova Fumada
description: Long-established Barceloneta institution representing the real Barcelona—cash only, no reservations, and some of the freshest seafood in the city.

name: Can Fisher
description: Beachfront Poblenou venue featuring fresh seafood and paella specialties with an authentic neighbourhood feel and proximity to the sea.

name: Morro Fi
description: Represents the contemporary vermut scene at its best, with quality tapas and impeccable house vermouth at multiple city locations.

name: Bar Canyí
description: High-quality tapas with a relaxed, welcoming vibe at this popular Sant Antoni neighbourhood bar.

name: Bar Bocata
description: Famous for its tortilla with caramelised onion; vintage charm and a loyal neighbourhood following in Sarrià-Sant Gervasi.

name: Oído
description: An informal Gràcia spot with classics given the chef's personal touch; relaxed atmosphere with consistently good cooking.

name: Bar Anita
description: Very good dishes that change with the seasons; one of Eixample Dreta's most reliable and honest tapas addresses.

name: Per Feina Per Plaer
description: Spacious Sarrià-Sant Gervasi venue with both quick and elaborate menu sections catering to every mood and schedule.

name: El Puestu
description: Comforting tapas made with good quality ingredients; a no-nonsense neighbourhood bar in Fort Pienc that delivers every time.

name: Tangana
description: Tasty tapas made with great ingredients and matched with good wines; a solid and convivial Gràcia address.

name: Bar Alegría
description: Renovated Eixample Esquerra space featuring the famous truffle bikini sandwich; the original Sant Antoni location of the Abellán group.

name: Bar La Plata
description: Operating since 1945 and still serving the same four tapas; an irreplaceable Gothic Quarter institution with zero pretension.

name: El Maravillas
description: Located on a charming Les Corts plaza with an oyster and cava offering that stands out from the neighbourhood crowd.

name: Rabbit's Bar
description: The quality of the tapas is excellent and the service is fast; a reliable upper-neighbourhood address in Sarrià-Sant Gervasi.

name: Bar Malasang
description: Very tasty food in a casual, fun Eixample vintage tapas bar that draws a loyal local crowd.

name: Tapeo
description: Interesting menu with well-known classics given interesting twists; popular with locals and visitors alike in the Born.

name: Bodega La Puntual
description: Authentic food made with excellent quality ingredients at this solid Born bodega; honest cooking without unnecessary embellishment.

name: Betlem Miscellánia Gastronomica
description: Perfect size for something casual; an Eixample Dreta spot that gets the balance between quality and informality exactly right.

name: La Esquinica
description: Great casual tapas at very low prices; an honest Nou Barris neighbourhood bar that punches well above its modest setting.

name: Can Maño
description: A down-to-earth Barceloneta tapas bar serving sardines in the traditional style; as authentic as the neighbourhood itself.

name: Bar Remedios
description: Cool atmosphere and tasty dishes to share at this funky Eixample Esquerra bar; a neighbourhood favourite with personality.

name: Alnorte
description: Wonderfully charming Gràcia bar specialising in northern Spanish food with traditional cider service; a joyful neighbourhood discovery.

name: Casa Inés
description: Cute, homely Gràcia bar with local Catalan dishes; the most neighbourhood-feeling spot on the street.

name: Bar Omar
description: Good seasonal tapas with top-notch ingredients; a reliable and unfussy modern tapas address in Sarrià-Sant Gervasi.

name: O Retorno
description: Generous, huge portions with octopus as the house specialty; a filling and satisfying Eixample Esquerra favourite.

name: Tapas 24
description: Famous gourmet tapas bar by Carles Abellán with the iconic truffle biquini sandwich; the most-replicated dish in Barcelona.

name: Entrepanes Diaz
description: Old-school Eixample Dreta aesthetic with bocata de calamares as the must-order specialty; a brilliant sandwich institution.

name: Bar Cañete
description: Quality seafood tapas at this well-regarded Raval institution on Carrer Unió; consistently one of the best bars in the area.

name: El Quim de la Boqueria
description: Delightful market tapas cooked to order inside the Boqueria Market; one of the most atmospheric eating spots in Barcelona.

name: Bar Bodega l'Electricitat
description: Wine poured straight from the barrel; a genuine old-timer Barceloneta spot that has changed almost nothing since it opened.

name: Quimet y Quimet
description: Always packed Poble Sec classic famous for the salmon and cream cheese tapa; a standing-room-only institution that never disappoints.

name: Bar Mut
description: Cosy and quite romantic smaller Eixample Dreta tapas bar; the place to go when you want intimacy without fuss.

name: Paco Meralgo
description: Good quality tapas in a well-regarded Eixample Esquerra restaurant; serious enough for a sit-down meal, relaxed enough for a quick stop.

name: Bambarol
description: Menu has a little creative twist and is not expensive; an enjoyable Sarrià-Sant Gervasi neighbourhood find.

name: Cal Pep
description: If you are into seafood, you must go to this Born classic; counter service and fresh fish define a decades-long Barcelona institution.

name: Hermós, Bar de Peix
description: Fantastic seafood dishes and natural wines inside the Llibertat market in Gràcia; one of the city's best market-counter experiences.

name: Fino Bar
description: Innovative, yet down-to-earth small plates at this Gràcia bar on Torrent de les Flors; Andalusian-Catalan flavours with a personal touch.

name: La Bombeta
description: Good quality tapas at this classic Barceloneta neighbourhood spot; famous for the bomba (fried meat croquette) that likely originated here.

name: Vaso de Oro
description: Barceloneta specialty is solomillo con foie, served alongside the house-brewed craft beer; a standing-room institution with character.

name: Bodega Quimet
description: Great neighbourhood atmosphere with excellent cured meats and cheeses at this classic Gràcia bodega wine bar.

name: Antunez
description: Friendly, laid-back Gràcia atmosphere with the duck and foie canelón as the signature dish worth ordering every time.

name: Extra Bar
description: Creative twist on tapas at this popular Gràcia spot; a cool, energetic bar that attracts a younger food-curious crowd.

name: Can Paixano
description: A true Barceloneta classic with cheap, quality cava and simple, tasty tapas; always heaving and always worth the jostle.

name: Bar Restaurant Ginés
description: Platters of shellfish and fish at good prices—'safates de marisc i peix a bon preu'—at this reliable neighbourhood seafood bar near Phenomena Cinema.

name: Bodega Can Ros
description: Historic establishment since 1971 serving calamars a la romana, bunyols de bacallà, and truites de patates near Cinema Phenomena.

name: Fukamura
description: Japanese omakase restaurant at Còrsega 479 offering a full chef's selection tasting menu; one of Barcelona's most ceremonial dining experiences.

name: Sartoria Panatieri
description: Named second-best pizzeria in the world in 2024; the Encarnació 51 address by Rafa Panatieri and Jorge Sastre is unmissable.

name: Fino Bar
description: Small plates and traditional fare at Torrent de les Flors 113; recommended as a pre- or post-cinema stop near Cinema Phenomena in Gràcia.

name: Arraval
description: Trendy Raval restaurant offering a modern, spice-forward reinterpretation of Catalan cuisine with bold, urban personality near La Filmoteca.

name: Assalto
description: Wine and Catalan small plates at Nou de la Rambla 44, with continuous kitchen service Friday through Monday near La Filmoteca.

name: Bar Cañete
description: Well-regarded Raval tapas bar at Carrer Unió 17; recommended as a top dining option near La Filmoteca cinema.

name: Casa Leopoldo
description: Historic Raval dining room at Sant Rafael 24; a long-standing institution in the neighbourhood that predates the area's gentrification.

name: Suculent
description: Contemporary Catalan cooking at Rambla del Raval 45; a quality dining option recommended for the area near La Filmoteca.

name: Louro
description: Best Galician gastronomy in an overlooked corner—'racó desconegut'—at the Centre Gallec on Rambla dels Caputxins; a genuine neighbourhood secret.

name: Finorri
description: Territory-focused Catalan restaurant at Hotel Condal, Boqueria 23; recommended near both Poliorama theatre and the Liceu opera house.

name: Sophie Kai
description: Japanese restaurant with Mediterranean touches at Rec Comtal 12; recently relaunched by the Banco de Boquerones group near Urquinaona.

name: Casa Xica
description: A creative blend of Asian and Catalan cooking—'cuina asiàtica i catalana'—at França Xica 20 in Poble Sec near Montjuïc venues.

name: Margarit
description: Greek and Mediterranean coastal cuisine—'cuina grega i de tota la llera del Mediterrani'—at Margarit 58 in Poble Sec.

name: Petit Montjuïc
description: Algerian-Catalan fusion cooking—'cuina algerianocatalana'—at Sant Isidre 2 in Poble Sec; a genuinely original combination unique to Barcelona.

name: Bornès
description: Traditional Catalan cuisine updated with contemporary cocktails at Carassa 2 in the Born, near the Museu Picasso.

name: Zaytun
description: An excellent gastronomic proposition—'proposta gastronòmica excel·lent'—at Guadiana 40 in Sants, near the Sala Flyhard theatre.

name: Tapas 24
description: Chef Carles Abellán describes himself as 'un home de barra enamorat de les tapes'; his iconic truffle biquini sandwich originated at El Bulli with Ferran Adrià.

name: Ultramarinos Marín
description: Owners Borja Garcia and Adria Castro prepare cured fish and grilled leg of lamb using refined techniques—curing, pickling, marinating, roasting—without unnecessary flourishes.

name: Fat Veggies
description: Run by Venezuelans Juan and Aquiles Martini using locally sourced organic vegetables and natural wines; 'like a cult, converting meat-eaters into devout veggie lovers.'

name: Buriti
description: Authentic Brazilian cuisine including moqueca, picanha, acarajés, and feijoada; 'a balm for saudade' with elevated quality and genuine professionalism.

name: Bruma
description: Created by Soma founders, this Aribau 31 restaurant 'fusiona la cocina española y la catalana' with minimalist presentation and accessible pricing.

name: Fidelio
description: Trendy, family-oriented trattoria at Beethoven 14 featuring New York-style pizza and pasta dishes; 'desenfadado' and very Instagrammable.

name: Garum
description: A neighbourhood restaurant offering 'elaboradísimas croquetas' and seasonal dishes, with a weekday lunch menu at €16 on Carrer de Vilarós.

name: Casa Chiquita
description: Terrace spot on Av. Diagonal 557 featuring classic tapas and vermut in a tranquil, unhurried atmosphere—'ambiente tranquilo'—in Les Corts.

name: Aüc Bar
description: By Joan Martínez and Takeshi Somekawa, honouring 'las tapas y los platos catalanes más icónicos' with local products at Plaça del Camp 7.

name: Fogón del Castellano y Leonés
description: Regional classics like torreznos and callos alongside contemporary dishes; daily menu €16 and weekend menu €27 at Passeig de Torras i Bages 139.

name: La Bodegueta de Sant Andreu
description: Recently awarded the Barcelona Restauració prize for neighbourhood integration; known for multiple croqueta varieties and artisanal local products on Rambla de Fabra i Puig.

name: L'Empanat
description: Combines traditional patatas bravas and eggs with charcoal Josper-oven hamburgers and seasonal offerings at Carrer de Monlau 60 in Sant Andreu.

name: L'Arrossat de l'Empanat
description: Specialises in paella variants and fideuà at Plaça de Masadas 1; 'each grain reflecting Mediterranean essence' according to the kitchen's philosophy.

name: La Marcelina
description: One Repsol Guide star; daily menu at €17.50; chef-led tribute to 'authentic, honest cooking' using proximity-sourced ingredients at Costa Rica 24.

name: Atipical
description: By Matteo Bertozzi; 'cocina con ingenio, técnica y sensibilidad'—one of the notable new openings of 2025 according to Barcelona food writers.

name: Pompa
description: Intimate, creative space by Carles Pérez de Rozas; the casual sibling of Berbena that became one of 2025's most talked-about openings.

name: Direkte
description: By Arnau Muñío; the relocated and expanded format of this much-praised Barcelona restaurant, now with greater ambition and larger premises on Carrer de París.

name: Agreste Mar
description: Mediterranean-inspired Italian-Catalan fusion at the Serras Barcelona Hotel; one of 2025's notable new dining openings in the Born area.

name: Arraval
description: Urban, spice-forward reinterpretation of traditional Catalan cooking; one of the most talked-about Raval openings of 2025.

name: Franca
description: Contemporary approach to regional Catalan cuisine; one of 2025's great openings that established itself as a serious special-occasion destination.

name: Finorri
description: Territory-focused Catalan restaurant that became popular almost immediately after opening; one of 2025's quietly successful newcomers.

name: Bornès
description: Updated traditional Catalan cuisine paired with contemporary cocktails; a neighbourhood Born restaurant that found its audience quickly in 2025.

name: Casa Fiero
description: Creative cocktails and neighbourhood energy; a 2025 opening that established itself as a lively, social address with distinctive character.

name: Fonda Balmes
description: A traditional Catalan restaurant on Balmes that grew steadily in popularity throughout 2025; solid neighbourhood cooking at honest prices.

name: Cerveseria 640
description: Impeccable execution of classic Spanish tapas and dishes; a 2025 opening that proved there is still room for traditional excellence in Barcelona.

name: Ultramarinos Marín
description: Hybrid bar, butcher, and charcuterie temple; representative of the charcuterie-forward dining trend that defined Barcelona's 2025 food scene.

name: Provisions Gresca
description: Curated cured meats by the team behind Gresca; a focused charcuterie specialist that became a key address in the 2025 bar-food revival.

name: Fukamura
description: A ceremonial Japanese omakase experience that became one of 2025's most-discussed new restaurants among Barcelona food writers.

name: Ippudo
description: The global ramen chain's Barcelona interpretation; widely noted among 2025's notable new openings for bringing serious Japanese ramen culture to the city.

name: Lora
description: Middle Eastern restaurant at SLS Barcelona that stood out as 2025's top brunch destination; inventive and sophisticated menu by a skilled team.

name: Fronda Pasaje
description: 'Un espacio on el menjar i el coneixement van de bracet'—a Barcelona space where food and knowledge walk together; a cultural-gastronomy hybrid.

name: Mineral
description: Project by Oliver Peña and Cristina Losada; a highly anticipated 2026 opening from two of Barcelona's most respected young chefs.

name: Orobianco Barcelona
description: Fine dining project with Paolo Casagrande (of Lasarte) as gastronomic director; one of the most anticipated openings of early 2026.

name: La Parada Torres
description: Collaboration between Sergio and Javier Torres opening at Mercat de Santa Caterina; a spring 2026 debut from two celebrated Catalan chefs.

name: Trü
description: Artur Martínez presents a short menu of Catalan recipes in tapas version with personal interpretation; features trinxat and mollejas at Còrsega 232.
notes: Listed as 'Aürt' in Jan 2026 article before final name confirmed; confirmed as Trü by Repsol March 2026

name: Masia del Tibidabo
description: Rafa Zafra's Tibidabo project focusing on seasonal, local ingredients in a landmark hilltop setting above the city.

name: Hermanos Alam's Taco
description: 'Un templo del taco al pastor' with specialised salsas; a Barcelona taquería bringing genuine Mexican taco culture to the city.

name: Finca Nebot
description: By Leo Chechelnitskiy; a Poblenou restaurant with emphasis on classic Catalan dishes and a commitment to product quality.

name: Super Auto
description: By Ronit Stern; a 2026 opening in Eixample that generated early buzz among Barcelona food writers and restaurant professionals.

name: Mercado Central
description: Cevichería by Pablo Ortega in Poble Sec; a focused Peruvian concept built around ceviche and the marine traditions of Lima.

name: Bottega Bernacca
description: By Gerard Barberan with Michelin-level experience from Brazil; a fusion project that blends European and South American culinary sensibilities.

name: Cresta Negra
description: Omar Malpartida's roasted chicken-focused Peruvian concept; a more casual and accessible counterpart to his other Barcelona projects.

name: Arcano
description: Occupies 17th-century former cathedral stables at Mercaders 10; chef Nicolás Limarino updates historic Catalan recipes—brioche with country sausage, braised beef cheeks in rancio wine—with modern technique.

name: Kiosko Universal
description: Over 50 years operating inside La Boqueria under third-generation management; fresh tuna tataki, razor clams with foie, and grilled Galician-style octopus from market stall to plate.

name: Sophie Kai
description: Recently relaunched by Banco de Boquerones group; intimate spaces with distinct colour zones; philosophy 'a Barcelona perspective on contemporary Japan: approachable, aesthetic, fresh and honest.' Gyoza, teriyaki skewers, and white chocolate matcha tiramisu.

name: Tapas Lobito
description: Recently opened by Grupo Tragaluz at Porxos d'en Xifré; bar concept combining Iberian ham croquettes and fried artichokes with a dedicated sushi counter and foosball table.

name: Jazminos
description: Small and welcoming Middle Eastern restaurant with an elegant approach and dinner menu under €30; a neighbourhood secret in Putxet.

name: Bar Fischer
description: Honest cooking at affordable prices with charming, attentive staff; a 2025 Eixample find that delivers reliable quality without pretension.

name: Vibeyard
description: Original menu with Portuguese touches and 10 house wines sourced directly from Lisbon vineyards; a singular natural wine bar in Gràcia.

name: Pompa
description: Short but sublime menu perfectly suited for couples or special dinners; one of 2025's best new openings in Gràcia for serious food lovers.

name: Arraval
description: Mediterranean design with 'reinterpreted Catalan dishes with personality'; one of the most admired new restaurants of 2025 in the Raval.

name: Franca
description: 'Curious and different' special-occasion restaurant; one of 2025's most distinctive openings for those seeking something genuinely original in Eixample.

name: Fiffty-Fiffty
description: Quality fast food better than the famous competitors; a Poblenou spot that elevated the burger-and-chips format with noticeably better ingredients.

name: Esnaf
description: Daily-changing healthy plates with a strong takeaway focus; a Poblenou spot that reflects the neighbourhood's growing creative-class appetite for honest, wholesome food.

name: Bar Veracruz
description: Historic décor preserved intact; known for generous fork breakfasts and excellent midday dishes that honour the classic Barcelona bar tradition.

name: Bodega Manolo
description: Classic Gràcia tavern with noticeably improved dishes under new ownership; the neighbourhood bodega format done with greater care and quality.

name: Bar Casi
description: Perfect for lunch; respects the historic character of its Gràcia space with honest traditional cooking and a loyal neighbourhood clientele.

name: Bodega Nulles
description: Authentic vermut selection with traditional tapas; one of 2025's recommended Eixample aperitif bars for a genuine vermouth ritual.

name: Bar Bruno
description: Small and welcoming Sarrià-Sant Gervasi wine bar with an excellent selection; the kind of neighbourhood bar you want to discover yourself.

name: Casa Fiero
description: Creative cocktails and a social, energetic atmosphere; one of 2025's most enjoyable new Eixample bars that quickly built a loyal crowd.

name: Trü
description: Chef Artur Martínez presents a short menu of Catalan recipes in tapas version with personal interpretation; features trinxat and mollejas at Còrsega 232.

name: Barra Oso
description: Óscar Álvarez's intimate ten-seat seafood bar at Muntaner 248 with candlelit atmosphere; focuses on fish dishes and creative preparations like cured serviola.

name: Bar Alegría
description: Tomás Abellán's Gràcia location at Torrent de l'olla 77, maintaining the '1970s allure' of the original; features ibérico pork, cockles, and Maresme peas with butifarra.

name: Barra M
description: Omar Malpartida's intimate ten-seat bar at Viladomat 249 'revindicating omakase through a deep Nikkei perspective' blending Japanese technique with vibrant Peruvian ingredients.

name: Taberna Nardi
description: Like a rowdy fish-market stall: fresh razor clams, mussels with parsley butter, and chargrilled red tuna neck in a tiny Born bar.

name: Cerveseria 640
description: Sister location to Colmado Wilmot offering classic tapas served fried, grilled, or raw with zero fuss in a spacious beer-hall setting.

name: Mundial Bar
description: Historic haunt relaunched as affordable seafood bar; dinner for two costs €50, with vintage literary and boxing memorabilia setting the scene.

name: Al Grano Bistro
description: Cosy Eixample spot featuring rich wild boar pappardelle, pillowy ossobuco ravioli, and house-made tiramisu; serious Italian cooking in an unfussy setting.

name: Finorri
description: Cantabrian anchovies, seasonal mushrooms, and rice bursting with fresh seafood; upscale Spanish-Catalan cooking in a Gothic Quarter setting.

name: Casa Fiero
description: 1970s diner throwback featuring French bistro classics and upscale tapas; known for the decadent, gratinated macaroni that keeps regulars coming back.

name: Bar Super
description: Sister to Bar Brutal; cured horsemeat cecina with wildly expressive cloudy orange wines from €4.50—the best value natural wine bar in the Santa Caterina area.

name: Paloma
description: Modern Mexican featuring green seabass aguachile with authentic Oaxacan cheese; serious Mexican flavours in an elegant Gràcia setting.

name: Pompa
description: Hake pil-pil made with cheeks and an exceptional cheese and charcuterie selection; the casual sibling of Berbena that has become a neighbourhood essential.

name: Chambacú
description: Tasting menu €55–€95; 'modern interpretation of Colombian cooking' fusing Indigenous, Spanish, and African flavours with ambition and technique.

name: Lora
description: Fattoush salad that crunches and bursts with freshness, and a shoulder of lamb glazed in pomegranate; the SLS Barcelona hotel restaurant that earned its place on the hit list.
